flow_chart: Generated flow chart showing interactions between crawlers

key_interdependencies.title: Shared Test Method in crawlService.js
key_interdependencies.description: The testCrawl method contains logic for both Puppeteer and API crawlers, selected by a 'method' parameter
key_interdependencies.risk: If method parameter is incorrectly set or defaults, it could trigger API calls when Puppeteer was intended

key_interdependencies.title: Error Propagation and Shared Error Handling
key_interdependencies.description: Both crawlers use similar error handling patterns, with 503 errors being common
key_interdependencies.risk: Errors in one system could be incorrectly attributed to the other

key_interdependencies.title: Module Loading Conflicts
key_interdependencies.description: The system has mixed CommonJS and ES Module syntax which could lead to unpredictable behavior
key_interdependencies.risk: Improper module loading may cause unexpected API calls or error logging

key_interdependencies.title: Error Reporting in UI
key_interdependencies.description: Error logs are currently using mock data that may not accurately distinguish between crawler types
key_interdependencies.risk: Users may see API errors even when Puppeteer was the only crawler used

why_api_errors_with_puppeteer: The testCrawl method in crawlService.js selects between API and Puppeteer based on method parameter
There may be implicit fallbacks to API methods when Puppeteer fails
Error logging may not accurately distinguish between error sources
Module conflicts between CommonJS and ES Modules may cause unexpected behavior

recommendation: Clearly separate the Puppeteer and API crawler implementations
Fix module conflicts by standardizing on one module system
Implement proper error source tracking in logs
Add explicit logging when method selection occurs
